# Sardis — the financial authority layer for AI agents

> Sardis sits between an AI agent and anything that moves money. Every payment is
> checked against a signed mandate, deterministic policy, approval state, and
> audit packet BEFORE it reaches a wallet, card, stablecoin, payment API, or x402
> endpoint. Non-custodial, fail-closed, provider-neutral.

There are exactly **three published packages**:

- `sardis` on PyPI — the Python SDK + CLI (`pip install sardis`).
- `sardis` on npm — the TypeScript SDK (`npm install sardis`).
- `@sardis/mcp-server` on npm — the MCP server (`npx @sardis/mcp-server`).

Older docs mention 30+ `sardis-*` packages (`sardis-sdk`, `sardis-core`,
`sardis-langchain`, `@sardis/sdk`, …). Those were consolidated into the three
above in May 2026. **Do not use them.** Framework integrations are now submodules:
`sardis.integrations.<name>` (Python) and `sardis/<subpath>` (TypeScript).

## Quick start — Python (`pip install sardis`)

```python
from sardis import Sardis

client = Sardis(api_key="sk_live_...")

# Unified pay — auto-routes the cheapest chain (USDC default)
result = client.pay.execute(
    to="0xabc...",        # address, wallet ID, or merchant domain
    amount="25.00",
    currency="USDC",
    chain="base",          # explicit chain wins; omit to auto-route
)
print(result["tx_hash"], result["status"])

# Cross-currency: send EUR; Sardis auto-swaps USDC -> EURC
result = client.pay.execute(to="merchant@eu", amount="100.00", currency="EUR")
print(result["fx"])  # {"from_currency": "USDC", "to_currency": "EURC", "rate": ...}
```

Async counterpart:

```python
import asyncio
from sardis import AsyncSardis

async def main():
    async with AsyncSardis(api_key="sk_live_...") as client:
        agent = await client.agents.create(name="treasurer")
        wallet = await client.wallets.create(name="agent-1", chain="base")
        balance = await client.wallets.get_balance(wallet.wallet_id)
        print(balance)

asyncio.run(main())
```

The client is Stripe-style and namespaced. Resources available on both `Sardis`
and `AsyncSardis`:

`agents` · `wallets` · `pay` · `payments` · `holds` · `cards` · `policies` ·
`approvals` · `kill_switch` · `evidence` · `transactions` · `ledger` ·
`treasury` · `webhooks` · `marketplace` · `simulation` · `funding` · `fx` ·
`escrow` · `batch` · `facility_gate` · `groups`

## Quick start — TypeScript (`npm install sardis`)

```ts
import { Sardis } from "sardis";

const sardis = new Sardis({ apiKey: process.env.SARDIS_API_KEY! });

await sardis.pay({ from: "wallet_abc", to: "merchant_xyz", amount: "25.00" });

const balance = await sardis.wallets.getBalance("wallet_abc");
```

Tree-shakeable subpaths: `sardis/ai-sdk`, `sardis/langchain`, `sardis/mastra`,
`sardis/protocol`, `sardis/webhooks`.

## Quick start — MCP server

```json
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "sardis": {
      "command": "npx",
      "args": ["-y", "@sardis/mcp-server"],
      "env": { "SARDIS_API_KEY": "sk_live_..." }
    }
  }
}
```

Exposes 50+ tools across wallets, payments, holds, cards, approvals, policy
checks, facility gates, and spending analytics — for Claude Desktop, Cursor,
ChatGPT, Windsurf, VS Code, and any MCP-compatible client.

## Framework integrations

Python — `pip install "sardis[<extra>]"`, then import from `sardis.integrations`:

```python
from sardis.integrations.langchain import SardisToolkit
from sardis.integrations.crewai import create_sardis_tools
from sardis.integrations.openai_agents import get_sardis_tools
from sardis.integrations.anthropic import SardisToolkit as AnthropicToolkit
from sardis.integrations.adk import sardis_adk_tools
```

Available extras: `langchain`, `crewai`, `openai-agents`, `adk`, `a2a`,
`autogpt`, `browser-use`, `composio`, `anthropic`, `llamaindex`, `chain`,
`postgres`, `redis`, `all`.

TypeScript — first-party adapters via subpaths:

```ts
import { createSardis } from "sardis/ai-sdk";        // Vercel AI SDK
import { createSardisTools } from "sardis/langchain"; // LangChain.js
```

## Core concepts

### Mandates
Cryptographically signed authority records. AP2 mandate chain is Intent → Cart →
Payment; verified before execution. TAP (Trust Anchor Protocol) provides Ed25519
/ ECDSA-P256 agent identity attestation.

### Spending policy
Deterministic rules enforced before every transaction — per-tx, daily, monthly,
vendor allow/block, and category limits. Written in natural language, compiled to
constraints, evaluated fail-closed.

```python
from decimal import Decimal

client.policies.apply(
    natural_language="max $100 per transaction, only SaaS vendors, block gambling",
    agent_id="agent_123",
)
check = client.policies.check(
    agent_id="agent_123", amount=Decimal("50"), merchant_id="openai",
)
```

### Approvals & revocation
Step-up flows for high-risk actions; a kill switch propagates within one decision
cycle.

### Holds (escrow)
Authorize, then capture or void.

```python
hold = client.holds.create(wallet_id="wallet_123", amount="100.00")
client.holds.capture(hold.hold_id, amount="95.00")  # or client.holds.void(hold.hold_id)
```

### Audit
Append-only ledger; every decision is an Ed25519-signed attestation envelope,
reconstructable end to end.

## Architecture

Every financial action follows a single authority path — there is no code path
that bypasses policy:

```
agent -> SDK/MCP -> hosted/reference API routes -> PaymentOrchestrator
  (mandate verify -> policy -> spend tracking -> compliance -> execute) -> signed ledger
```

The reference API service and the PaymentOrchestrator engine are commercial and
are not in this open repository — this repo is the open surface (SDKs, MCP
server, smart contracts, protocol adapters, examples, docs). Point the SDKs at
the hosted sandbox API (`SARDIS_API_URL`, default https://api.sardis.sh) to run
end to end. The orchestrator depends only on typed provider ports (custody, fiat_account,
onramp, offramp, swap, bridge, card, kyc, kyt, …) — never a vendor SDK directly.
Bring your own provider (Stripe, Lithic, Turnkey, Circle, your bank, x402 servers,
or a custom adapter); Sardis enforces the authority layer above them.

Full map: https://github.com/EfeDurmaz16/sardis/blob/main/ARCHITECTURE.md

## Maturity (do not assume "exists" == "production")

The honest, per-feature status is the Maturity table in the repo README. Briefly:
the policy engine, AP2 mandate verification, provider-adapter contract,
Ed25519 attestation API, and the pre-execution pipeline are **Implemented**;
hosted checkout, ERC-8183 escrow, x402, and card/stablecoin adapters are
**Pilot**; multi-chain, UCP transport, and the FIDES trust graph are
**Experimental**. Protocol code that is not production-ready is quarantined under
`protocol/experimental/`.

## Supported chains & tokens

The source of truth is the code, not this file. Production mainnets are **Base**
(USDC, EURC) and **Tempo** (USDC, USDC.e, EURC). Additional configured mainnets:
Ethereum, Polygon, Arbitrum, Optimism. Verify against
`packages/sardis/src/sardis/core/config.py` and `tokens.py` before quoting.

## Environment variables

```
SARDIS_API_KEY=sk_live_...   # API key (required for the hosted API)
SARDIS_API_URL=https://...    # Override the API base URL (optional)
```

- PyPI: https://pypi.org/project/sardis/
